steps.title: Identify the Paradox
steps.content:
The passage sets up a contrast: CLPs are shielded from the external environment by their transmission strategy, while directly transmitted parasites are exposed to external conditions.

Yet the data shows the opposite of what we'd expect: CLP abundance decreased with warming, while directly transmitted parasite abundance remained stable.

This contradiction is the key signal—the shielding advantage didn't prevent decline.

steps.title: What the Data Really Suggests
steps.content:
CLPs' transmission strategy shields them, yet they still declined with warming—meaning their shielding advantage is real but insufficient against other temperature-driven factors.

Choice A directly states this: advantages exist but “did not completely offset the negative effects of other temperature-driven factors.” The other three either invent unsupported distinctions (B), contradict the data by claiming more hosts improve temperature protection (C), or claim directly transmitted parasites have shielding when the passage explicitly says they're exposed (D).

That matches choice A — A is correct.
